title: 4chan
description: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

items.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Hector (Release date TBD)
items.content_text: >Hector (Noel Gugliemi) served his country as an Army Ranger in Iraq and Afghanistan. Now, all he wants is a quiet life in Los Angeles—running his auto repair shop, grilling in the backyard, and raising his family. But when a ruthless South American cartel affiliate rolls into the neighborhood aiming to claim the block through fear and violence, Hector’s peaceful retirement comes to a dead stop.

items.title: /v/ Video Games: Trails in the Sky
items.content_text: Seriously, what's the point? As a newcomer, why should I bother with this game when it's part of a multi-series saga and nearly every event (if the memes are to be believed) is almost inconsequential anyway. It's like reading the entire LotR trilogy only to be told everything was just a footnote in the "real" story. Sauron was just a pawn and Frodo now has to carry an even deadlier, more evil ring to an even more imposing lava mountain. And this whole shit escalates and repeats ad infinitum. I r

items.title: /v/ Video Games: Capcom Survey: We want Jill back and Ada out.
items.content_text: Reminder: Resident Evil doesn't need more terrorists pretending to be heroes. Let Ada be treated as what she has always been: a compelling villain, not a role model. Jill, Claire, Rebecca and Sheva carried the franchise through courage, sacrifice, professionalism and genuine heroism. If you think Resident Evil lost part of its identity by glorifying morally ambiguous characters instead of its actual heroes, now is the time to let Capcom know. Many fans believe RE6 marked the point where the seri

items.title: /mu/ Music: The Chairman on Reprise is a study in why artists shouldn't own record companies…
items.content_text: The Chairman on Reprise is a study in why artists shouldn't own record companies. My researches into a catalogue that runs to some 100 LPs have yet to uncover a single title that comes near the great Capitols, and the compilations are not to be trusted. So rather than spending $60 for the choppy 81-song box, try this 20-song oddity, supposedly programmed by Frank himself at age 79 and duplicating only seven box selections. It anoints more Don Costas than Nelson Riddles and is surprisingly scant

items.content_text: Hi, I'm looking for a version of this song that's just lost. It's not for me, it's for someone I love. She told me she discovered this song on Instagram and listened to the whole song on youtube. There were three videos of that version, all official and with fewer than 100 views, but for some reason they were under the name of Doris Ray.
